steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: The line has slope $-\frac{1}{3}$, so its equation must look like $$y=-\frac{x}{3}+b.$$ That immediately eliminates choices A and B because their slopes are not $-\frac{1}{3}$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The equation is $$y=-\frac{x}{3}+13.$$ A Desmos table gives the same result: start with $(9,10)$, then use the slope $-\frac{1}{3}$ to get another point $(12,9)$. two-point line calculation through those two points returns $y=-\frac{x}{3}+13$. Choice D is correct. Choice C has the right slope but the wrong intercept; it gives $y=7$ when $x=9$, not $10$.
